diary:
Woke up early in Queen Anne with that sunny Monday feeling that makes everything seem fixable. After a quick shower, I grabbed breakfast essentials at home and then drifted over toward Pike Place Market for an iced drink and a bagel. The air there always smells like coffee and seawater—like the city’s just waking up with me. Work at SoFi on Pike Street was a solid stretch of backend stuff and code reviews, plus standup and planning that somehow always runs longer than I think.

I noticed the light rail closure between Westlake and University District, so I adjusted my route and felt oddly grateful I’d planned ahead. By the time I got back, USA vs Wales was on—highlights while I unpacked groceries from Whole Foods and QFC. Later, I walked over to Gas Works Park to reset, then met a small group at Top Pot Doughnuts for dessert and easy conversation. Now I’m back on the couch, tired in a good way, pleasantly content.
thought_process: I tried to keep the morning low-friction: coffee and food near Pike Place so I wouldn’t waste energy before work, then a straightforward commute along the downtown transit-friendly core. The light rail closure actually nudged my thinking—rather than getting annoyed, I rerouted early and treated it like a small puzzle, which helped my mood. At work I focused on backend tasks and reviews because that’s where I can make steady progress, and standup/planning felt easier once I’d already mentally lined up my priorities. The World Cup match became my “reward checkpoint,” not a distraction—highlights let me enjoy it without losing the day. By evening, after Gas Works Park, my head felt clearer, and the doughnut hangout smoothed out the edges into something calm.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm.
